mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days when policy is moving lower in a sustained way.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline after FEDFUNDS has fallen for 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if the sign of the conditional return is negative in a majority of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the prior 3-month trend while FEDFUNDS is still below the inflation pace, real policy becomes more accommodative on an ex post basis and equities rally over the next 5 trading days on reduced real-rate pressure.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2pp annualized above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the 5d forward return after inflation-surprise triggers is not positive on average, or if the effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is at or above CPI inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and future demand, so sustained increases in unemployment lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward returns are positive in most episodes where UNRATE has risen at least 0.3pp in 3 months, or if the conditional mean return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE indicates broad labor-market strengthening that supports consumption and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE do not beat unconditional 60d SPX returns, or if the sign turns negative when both labor indicators strengthen together.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer demand and revenue momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth exceeds its 6-month average by at least 2%
failure_condition: Reject if high-RSAFS-growth episodes fail to deliver above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate is below 50% over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in INDPRO captures stronger industrial output and cyclical earnings leverage; when production is rising, S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO upswings are followed by negative or zero-average 60d SPX returns, or if the directional accuracy falls below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after a prior drop signals stabilizing credit-sensitive demand and improved cyclical sentiment, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST turns up after a decline of at least 10% over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if housing-rebound triggers are followed by non-positive 5d SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the mean return after triggers is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth remains positive while CPI inflation is stable and FEDFUNDS is not rising, the economy is in a soft-landing regime that supports equity multiples and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined growth-plus-stable-inflation condition does not outperform baseline 20d returns, or if the effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising despite positive GDPC1 growth.
